2603.26592.title: Evaluating Interactive 2D Visualization as a Sample Selection Strategy for Biomedical Time-Series Data Annotation
2603.26592.paper_summary: Reliable machine-learning models in biomedical settings depend on accurate labels, yet annotating biomedical time-series data remains challenging. Algorithmic sample selection may support annotation, but evidence from studies involving real human annotators is scarce. Consequently, we compare three sample selection methods for annotation: random sampling (RND), farthest-first traversal (FAFT), and a graphical user interface-based method enabling exploration of complementary 2D visualizations (2DVs) of high-dimensional data. We evaluated the methods across four classification tasks in infant motility assessment (IMA) and speech emotion recognition (SER). Twelve annotators, categorized as experts or non-experts, performed data annotation under a limited annotation budget, and post-annotation experiments were conducted to evaluate the sampling methods. Across all classification tasks, 2DV performed best when aggregating labels across annotators. In IMA, 2DV most effectively captured rare classes, but also exhibited greater annotator-to-annotator label distribution variability resulting from the limited annotation budget, decreasing classification performance when models were trained on individual annotators' labels; in these cases, FAFT excelled. For SER, 2DV outperformed the other methods among expert annotators and matched their performance for non-experts in the individual-annotator setting. A failure risk analysis revealed that RND was the safest choice when annotator count or annotator expertise was uncertain, whereas 2DV had the highest risk due to its greater label distribution variability. Furthermore, post-experiment interviews indicated that 2DV made the annotation task more interesting and enjoyable. Overall, 2DV-based sampling appears promising for biomedical time-series data annotation, particularly when the annotation budget is not highly constrained.
2603.26592.paper_summary_zh: 可靠的機器學習模型在生物醫學環境中依賴於準確的標籤，但標註生物醫學時間序列數據仍然具有挑戰性。算法樣本選擇可能支持標註，但涉及真實人類標註者的研究證據稀少。因此，我們比較了三種標註樣本選擇方法：隨機抽樣 (RND)、最遠優先遍歷 (FAFT) 和一種基於圖形用戶界面的方法，該方法能夠探索高維數據的互補 2D 可視化 (2DVs)。我們在嬰兒運動評估 (IMA) 和語音情感識別 (SER) 的四個分類任務中評估了這些方法。十二名標註者，分為專家和非專家，在有限的標註預算下進行數據標註，並進行了標註後實驗以評估樣本選擇方法。在所有分類任務中，2DV 在聚合標籤方面表現最佳。在 IMA 中，2DV 最有效地捕捉了稀有類別，但也顯示出由於有限的標註預算而導致的標註者之間標籤分佈變異性更大，當模型在單個標註者的標籤上進行訓練時，降低了分類性能；在這些情況下，FAFT 表現優異。對於 SER，2DV 在專家標註者中優於其他方法，並在個別標註者設置中與非專家的表現相匹配。一項失敗風險分析顯示，當標註者數量或標註者專業知識不確定時，RND 是最安全的選擇，而 2DV 由於其更大的標籤分佈變異性而具有最高的風險。此外，實驗後的訪談表明，2DV 使標註任務變得更有趣和愉快。總的來說，基於 2DV 的抽樣在生物醫學時間序列數據標註中顯示出良好的前景，特別是在標註預算不受嚴格限制的情況下。
2603.26592.author: Einari Vaaras et.al.
2603.26592.authors: Einari Vaaras, Manu Airaksinen, Okko Räsänen
